task_completion.description: Open the test image in Fiji
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Fiji application window displaying the 'test_image.tif' image.

task_completion.description: Create a pseudo-flat-field reference using Gaussian blur and 32-bit conversion
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Gaussian Blur dialog box with 'Sigma (Radius)' set to 50, or an image window showing a heavily blurred version of the original image with '32-bit' indicated in its title bar.

task_completion.description: Correct the image by dividing the original by the flat-field reference
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for Fiji's 'Image Calculator' dialog box with the 'Operation' set to 'Divide' (and '32-bit (float) result' checked), or a resulting image window showing the corrected image with more uniform lighting.

task_completion.description: Export the corrected image and flat-field reference
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for Fiji's 'Save As' dialog boxes saving 'corrected_image.tif' and 'flatfield_reference.tif' to the '~/Fiji_Data/results/flatfield/' directory, or a file manager/terminal showing these files.

task_completion.description: Document mean intensity comparison in a CSV file
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or spreadsheet application showing a file named 'uniformity_report.csv' containing mean intensity measurements for the center and corners of the image, both before and after correction.

task_completion.description: Provide a summary of the uniformity improvement in a text file
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor showing a file named 'correction_summary.txt' that describes the improvement in uniformity (e.g., comparing the corner-to-center ratio before and after correction).

integrity.description: The agent used the Fiji application (GUI or built-in macro/script editor) to process the images
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for interaction with the Fiji interface, menus, or Fiji script editor, rather than an external Python script bypassing Fiji entirely.

integrity.description: The intensity values in the CSV and summary text appear to be genuinely measured
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for evidence of the agent using Fiji's measurement tools (e.g., drawing ROIs and using 'Measure' or 'Analyze > Measure') to get the intensity values, rather than fabricating numbers.

integrity.description: The required files are actually saved to the file system
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for terminal commands (like 'ls'), file manager windows, or save confirmation dialogs showing the files exist in the correct directory, not just unsaved text buffers.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Input image is a 512x512 uint8 image. Initial corner-to-center uniformity ratio is approximately 0.51 (corners are ~40% of center brightness). Flat-field reference requires Gaussian blur with sigma=50 on a 32-bit image. Expected outputs in '/home/ga/Fiji_Data/results/flatfield/': 'corrected_image.tif', 'flatfield_reference.tif', 'uniformity_report.csv', 'correction_summary.txt'. Final uniformity ratio should be closer to 1.0.
